thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+6.56% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Exit matched entry exactly, meaning the scheduler fired with no elapsed price movement — no intraday thesis test occurred. The $1407.61 price for SNDK is anomalous and almost certainly reflects a data integrity issue with the instrument.
risks_observed: Nine identical SNDK positions were opened on the same session, indicating a trade-generation bug producing severe unintended single-name concentration. If the inflated price feeds into notional sizing, exposure is dramatically overstated relative to any reasonable limit.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.51% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Flat scheduled exit — price action between entry and close was either zero or not captured, so there is no signal on whether the gap held or reverted. FDS is a lower-beta financial data name; a +4.51% open gap is meaningful but intraday follow-through remains unobserved.
risks_observed: Nine duplicate FDS positions were generated, pointing to the same order-duplication fault seen across the whole basket. Even at normal liquidity, stacking nine identical lots amplifies open-print slippage and creates a position that no single-name limit framework would sanction.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.38% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Scheduled exit at entry price — zero price capture means momentum continuation was neither confirmed nor denied. APP is a high-beta adtech name where post-gap continuation is plausible but so is rapid mean-reversion; neither outcome is observable from this record.
risks_observed: Nine APP positions open simultaneously creates significant single-name concentration in a volatile, news-sensitive stock. APP's amplified correlation with broader risk-on/risk-off moves means a sector drawdown hits this name disproportionately, and no stop-loss is in evidence.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.29% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Exit at entry with no movement captured; the scheduled close fired before any reflectable intraday change. SWKS as a semiconductor name is sensitive to macro and sector headlines, but this trade yields no data on that dynamic.
risks_observed: Nine duplicate SWKS positions join identical duplications in every other basket name — if real, the aggregate book is inflated 9x across all five tickers, which would constitute a gross breach of any position-limit rule and expose the strategy to correlated drawdown with no offset.

thesis: NVIDIA initiated a 7.79M-share position in Coherent Corp (filed 2026-05-15), a direct institutional endorsement of COHR's role in AI optical networking infrastructure. RSI 63.3 is not overbought, volume is at-average (1.0x) rather than exhausted, suggesting the market is still absorbing the NVIDIA signal rather than having already fully priced it.
outcome_notes: Price was unchanged from entry to scheduled exit, indicating the NVIDIA filing (disclosed 2026-05-15, two days before entry) had already been absorbed by the market before the position was opened. No directional follow-through materialised during the hold window.
risks_observed: Entering two calendar days after the catalyst filing is the core failure mode here — the news-to-entry lag was long enough for institutional fast-money to have already acted. This position was also duplicated in the same session (see 4b7a7798), doubling COHR concentration on an identical signal without any new information.

thesis: Three simultaneous analyst upgrades with raised price targets on 2026-05-15 (B of A to $440, Barclays to $412, RBC to $435 vs current $370.94) represent coordinated sell-side conviction in Vertiv's AI data-centre infrastructure story; consensus targets imply 11–19% upside from here with RSI 69.3 leaving room before overbought territory.
outcome_notes: Flat exit at the scheduled time with no intraday price response to the triple-upgrade cluster, which had also been published two days prior. The window between catalyst and entry was again sufficient for the market to fully reprice, leaving no edge.
risks_observed: Same-day duplicate position in VRT (228912b0) created unintended 2x concentration in an already RSI-elevated name; if the stock had reversed, losses would have been doubled. VRT and COHR are both AI-infrastructure plays held simultaneously, adding sector-level correlation to the basket.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.29% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.20% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Scheduled exit at entry — no price movement registered, consistent with a same-bar or zero-duration hold. WDAY's gap likely reflected earnings or guidance news; whether that catalyst had intraday follow-through is not assessable from this record.
risks_observed: Nine duplicate WDAY entries mirror the pattern across all five tickers, strongly suggesting a systemic trade-generation fault rather than intentional sizing. The basket's five-name tech concentration (adtech, SaaS, semiconductors, storage, financial data) means correlation in a risk-off session would be high with no diversification benefit.

thesis: XLE is the clear sector leader on both 5d (+6.71%) and 20d (+8.03%) relative strength, with technicals confirming: RSI 59 (not overbought), bullish MACD histogram, and price well above both SMA20 and SMA50. Higher oil prices are the catalyst this week; macro view is neutral on energy but price action overrides when momentum is this clean.
outcome_notes: The scheduled exit closed the position at exactly the entry price, meaning the oil-driven momentum thesis never converted into realized price movement within the holding window. No directional follow-through developed — either the catalyst was already priced in at entry or the move occurred outside the scheduled hold period.
risks_observed: A flat scheduled exit exposes the strategy's structural dependency on timing: if momentum runs before or after the holding window, the edge is never captured. Single-ETF energy concentration also means any surprise crude inventory print or OPEC headline could have produced an asymmetric loss with no intra-basket offset.

thesis: XLK leads all sectors on 20d relative strength (+14.20%) with macro explicitly bullish on AI capex and semis; elevated volume ratio (1.32) confirms institutional participation. RSI at 73 is stretched so sizing is trimmed and TP widened to let the secular trend run; Friday's rate-fear dip is treated as noise against the dominant uptrend.
outcome_notes: An RSI-73 entry into a rate-fear dip likely stalled the immediate upside, and the scheduled exit locked in a flat result before the secular AI/semis trend could reassert. The trimmed sizing was appropriate given stretch, but the holding window was too short to distinguish noise from a genuine reversal.
risks_observed: Holding both XLE and XLK simultaneously creates a momentum-factor concentration at the basket level — both positions were selected on relative-strength signals, so a broad risk-off rotation would have hit both legs together with no hedge. The RSI-73 entry also left minimal buffer before a stop would be threatened on any intraday volatility spike.

thesis: NVIDIA disclosed a new 7,788,161-share strategic stake in Coherent Corp on 2026-05-15 — a direct endorsement from the dominant AI-infrastructure company for its optical-transceiver supplier; earnings risk is already past (reported 2026-05-06) and RSI at 63 signals the market is still digesting the news rather than fully pricing it.
outcome_notes: Identical outcome to 6ad9bcf1 — flat scheduled exit on the same ticker, same price, same session. The position added no incremental exposure beyond the duplicate; it effectively just doubled notional on a signal that had already gone stale.
risks_observed: Duplicate-firing on the same catalyst is a strategy-level defect: the deduplication guard either failed or is absent. Running two full-size positions in COHR simultaneously inflated single-name concentration to levels that would have been painful had a sharp adverse move occurred.

thesis: BofA ($440), Barclays ($412), and RBC ($435) all raised price targets on the same day with Vertiv at $370.94, well below all three consensus targets; a same-day triple-upgrade cluster from top-tier banks is a concrete, positive catalyst and the 9% five-day move leaves meaningful room to the consensus PT range.
outcome_notes: Flat at scheduled exit, mirroring 09459e9c exactly. No incremental price discovery occurred during the hold, consistent with a catalyst that had been fully discounted before the position was initiated.
risks_observed: The basket on this date carried four positions across only two AI-infrastructure names (COHR ×2, VRT ×2) plus one energy name, meaning roughly 80% of deployed capital was correlated to the same AI data-centre theme. A single adverse macro headline (rate spike, export-control news) could have hit both names simultaneously.

thesis: A confluence of oil-specific macro catalysts — Jeff Currie's commodity supercycle call, Bessent's Hormuz reopening signal, and reports of well-timed pre-announcement oil bets — is driving volume 1.68x the 20-day average into XOM with RSI at 59.6 still below overbought, suggesting institutional positioning in progress.
outcome_notes: Exited flat at the scheduled time despite above-average volume on entry, suggesting the elevated volume reflected broad market participation rather than sustained directional conviction in XOM specifically. The macro catalysts cited (Currie call, Hormuz signal) are slow-moving and may not generate a single-session price move.
risks_observed: Macro-narrative catalysts (supercycle thesis, geopolitical signals) are inherently diffuse and hard to time — they argue for a multi-day hold, not an intraday or short scheduled window, so the exit structure was mismatched to the thesis. XOM provided sector diversification from the AI names but was the only energy position, leaving the strategy's non-AI leg thin.

thesis: Technology is macro-rated bullish on the AI capex supercycle; NVIDIA's fresh 7.8M-share stake in Coherent is a direct, high-conviction catalyst confirming the photonics/optical-interconnect bottleneck narrative. RSI 63.3 and an expanding MACD histogram (+4.68) leave room to run without being overbought, and price is well clear of both SMAs.
outcome_notes: The position was closed at the scheduled time with no price movement captured. The NVIDIA stake catalyst did not generate intraday follow-through during the holding window, suggesting the announcement was already absorbed into price at entry — the signal was real but the timing was late relative to when the market priced it.
risks_observed: Single-catalyst dependence on a disclosed institutional stake creates event-risk concentration: once the filing is public, the edge is thin and the window short. Both COHR and VRT in the same basket share AI-capex exposure, so any macro or sentiment reversal in that theme would have hit both legs simultaneously with no natural hedge.

thesis: Industrials are mildly bullish via reshoring/infrastructure spend, and Vertiv's AI data-centre power and cooling infrastructure gives it a direct co-exposure to the same capex cycle as tech; three analyst upgrades this week (BofA $440, RBC $435, Barclays $412 vs $371 close) confirm the fundamental re-rating. RSI 69.3 and positive MACD support continued momentum with the $440 BofA target as the natural profit objective.
outcome_notes: Closed flat at the scheduled exit, capturing none of the analyst-upgrade re-rating. Three upgrades in a single week often signals a coordinated view that is already partially priced by the time retail and systematic flows arrive; the $440 BofA target remained a valid destination but required a time horizon the scheduled exit could not accommodate.
risks_observed: RSI at 69.3 on entry left almost no buffer before overbought territory — any intraday rotation out of industrials would have pushed the position into the red before the scheduled close, with no stop in place to limit it. Correlation with COHR in the same session means the basket had two correlated AI-infrastructure longs with a combined unhedged sector bet.

thesis: Energy is the strongest sector this week (+6.71% 5d) and the commodity supercycle narrative is gaining traction in news flow; TRGP's midstream infrastructure model delivers energy exposure with stable, fee-based cash flows that are less sensitive to oil price volatility, backed by RSI 69.6, expanding MACD, and the highest volume ratio (1.49x) among energy candidates.
outcome_notes: Exited at the scheduled time with no gain despite entering the week's top-performing sector. A +6.71% five-day sector move often precedes a consolidation day rather than extension; the scheduled exit removed any ability to distinguish between a pause and a reversal, locking in neither profit nor a defensive cut.
risks_observed: Entering a momentum name after a multi-day run means price can sit flat or retrace even when the underlying thesis is intact — the fee-based model reduces oil-price sensitivity but not entry-timing risk. The highest volume ratio in the energy screen (1.49x) could equally indicate distribution rather than accumulation; without a volume-direction check, it was an ambiguous signal.

thesis: Best pure momentum signal in the set: 5-day +14.74%, RSI 70.7 (approaching but not over the 75 avoid threshold), MACD histogram expanding at +3.40, and volume running 1.29x the 20-day average — all on no adverse news.
outcome_notes: Slot was cleared (clear-slot ran) before this trade reached a natural exit. Either it was cancelled before any fill, or any real position was liquidated by clear-slot.
risks_observed: P&L not recorded because the actual fill/close price wasn't captured cleanly by clear-slot. Wave 2c reflection can flag this as data missing rather than a real outcome.

thesis: Strongest volume in the midstream energy cluster at 1.49x average, RSI 69.6 with room to run, MACD histogram expanding, and 20-day return of +15.49% confirms an established trend rather than a one-day spike; commodity supercycle narrative provides macro tailwind.
outcome_notes: Slot was cleared (clear-slot ran) before this trade reached a natural exit. Either it was cancelled before any fill, or any real position was liquidated by clear-slot.

thesis: NVIDIA disclosing a new 7.8M-share stake is a high-quality institutional catalyst; RSI 63.3 leaves meaningful upside before overbought territory, and the MACD histogram is expanding cleanly at +4.68 on a 5-day gain of +14.08%.
outcome_notes: Slot was cleared (clear-slot ran) before this trade reached a natural exit. Either it was cancelled before any fill, or any real position was liquidated by clear-slot.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+6.56% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Exit matched entry exactly, meaning the scheduler fired with no elapsed price movement — no intraday thesis test occurred. The $1407.61 price for SNDK is anomalous and almost certainly reflects a data integrity issue with the instrument.
risks_observed: Nine identical SNDK positions were opened on the same session, indicating a trade-generation bug producing severe unintended single-name concentration. If the inflated price feeds into notional sizing, exposure is dramatically overstated relative to any reasonable limit.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.51% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Flat scheduled exit — price action between entry and close was either zero or not captured, so there is no signal on whether the gap held or reverted. FDS is a lower-beta financial data name; a +4.51% open gap is meaningful but intraday follow-through remains unobserved.
risks_observed: Nine duplicate FDS positions were generated, pointing to the same order-duplication fault seen across the whole basket. Even at normal liquidity, stacking nine identical lots amplifies open-print slippage and creates a position that no single-name limit framework would sanction.

outcome_notes: Scheduled exit at entry — no price movement registered, consistent with a same-bar or zero-duration hold. WDAY's gap likely reflected earnings or guidance news; whether that catalyst had intraday follow-through is not assessable from this record.

thesis: European Energy ETF aligned with the top-ranked macro theme — oil +27.7% 20d, sector +7.15% 20d, holding above both SMA20 and SMA50 with RSI 60 (momentum without being overbought); the mild 5d pullback (-0.10%) into MACD support is a clean entry within an intact uptrend.
outcome_notes: Price was unchanged at exit, confirming neither the uptrend continuation nor any reversal — the position was simply held through the scheduled window without a price catalyst. The scheduled exit prevented any capture of the momentum the thesis anticipated, suggesting the holding period was too short relative to the macro driver's cycle. No information was generated about whether the MACD support level was meaningful.
risks_observed: Energy ETFs carry sharp reversal risk if oil momentum stalls or geopolitical supply narrative shifts intraday — a flat exit masks the fact that no stop was tested, not that no risk existed. Single-commodity-linked sector concentration means correlation to oil swings is essentially unhedged at the basket level, and both positions here share European-listed exposure, compounding EUR FX drag risk.

thesis: Defensive sector (likely Healthcare) fitting the stagflationary rotation backdrop where defensives are re-asserting; elevated volume ratio (1.90x avg) signals institutional interest and the MACD histogram (+0.309) is expanding with RSI at 52 — room to run vs the more extended Energy position.
outcome_notes: Flat exit on schedule with no price movement; the elevated volume ratio that flagged institutional interest did not translate into observable price action during the holding window, so the signal remains unconfirmed. The MACD expansion thesis was not tested — the scheduled exit pre-empted any read on whether the setup was leading or lagging.
risks_observed: A 1.90x volume spike is ambiguous without knowing the direction of that volume — if it was distribution rather than accumulation, the entry was into supply rather than demand. At the basket level, holding both a momentum-Energy and a defensive-Healthcare position simultaneously dilutes the rotation conviction: if stagflation is the thesis, sizing should reflect a cleaner directional view rather than hedging within the book.

thesis: ABN AMRO confirmed an analyst earnings beat on 2026-05-16, reversing prior misses; price is up 9.4% over 5 days on 1.79× average volume, indicating institutional re-rating is still in progress with next earnings not until August — classic post-beat drift setup.
outcome_notes: Price went nowhere between entry and the scheduled close, returning to the exact entry print. The post-beat drift either had already exhausted itself in the prior 5-day run before this session opened, or failed to extend on the day — the scheduled exit gave no room to distinguish between the two.
risks_observed: Entering after a 9.4% / 5-day move creates late-entry timing risk: the institutional re-rating may have already cleared rather than being mid-process. A time-based exit with no intraday profit target means any brief directional spike was left uncaptured, and there was no stop to limit downside had price reversed instead of going flat.

thesis: Fresh Strait of Hormuz crisis coverage (2026-05-17) plus a new Eastern Mediterranean deep-offshore gas MOU with Egypt give TotalEnergies a dual catalyst — supply disruption premium and strategic gas expansion — that has not yet fully lifted volume off its below-average base, leaving room for catch-up.
outcome_notes: Neither the Hormuz supply-risk premium nor the Egypt MOU translated into price movement during the holding window — the stock closed at exactly its entry level. Below-average volume at entry, flagged as a potential catch-up signal, instead indicated lack of conviction; the catalysts may have been too diffuse or already partially priced into the energy complex broadly.
risks_observed: Geopolitical headline trades are highly correlated with sector-wide energy moves, so if crude and the broader E&P basket didn't re-rate on the day, a single-name long had no independent lift. The dual-catalyst framing also risks overfitting to narrative — two simultaneous news drivers can mask the absence of a clean, measurable edge on either one.

thesis: Cleanest expression of the Bullish energy macro call: oil +27.7% (20d), Hormuz crisis reshaping global LNG flows, and TotalEnergies expanding Eastern Mediterranean gas exposure all directly support earnings. RSI 56 leaves room to run, MACD just turning positive, and both SMAs confirm the uptrend without the overbought risk seen in other candidates.
outcome_notes: Position closed flat at schedule with zero price movement captured — the macro tailwinds (oil spike, LNG re-routing) did not translate into intraday TTE.PA price action during the hold window. Scheduled exit fired before the thesis had time to express itself; no directional signal confirmed or invalidated.
risks_observed: A scheduled exit with no stop means the position had no downside protection if oil reversed intraday — the 27.7% 20-day run in crude already prices in significant crisis premium, leaving the stock exposed to a sharp unwind on any de-escalation headline. Holding a Hormuz-narrative position through a fixed schedule rather than a catalyst-driven exit increases gap risk around news flow.

thesis: Health care is Mildly Bullish as defensive rotation re-asserts in a stagflationary regime where consumers are squeezed and rate-sensitives are under pressure. Merck KGaA offers quality defensive exposure with an accelerating MACD histogram (+0.843), RSI 56 (ample room), and no overbought risk despite the broader market selling off on oil/yield concerns.
outcome_notes: Flat exit at schedule; defensive-rotation thesis produced no measurable price response within the hold window. With entry and exit at identical levels, there is no evidence the stagflationary bid for healthcare quality names was active on this session.
risks_observed: Defensive rotation trades are regime-dependent and slow-moving — a scheduled short-duration exit is structurally mismatched to that thesis, creating a pattern where the position absorbs any intraday noise without capturing the multi-day drift it was sized for. Correlation risk with the rest of the basket is low, but the zero-move outcome suggests broader market conditions were not conducive to any of the four positions.

thesis: Financials are Neutral but ABN AMRO carries a clear stock-specific catalyst: a just-reported analyst forecast beat confirmed by 1.79x above-average volume, which distinguishes it from the sector-level drift. The yield-curve steepening (10Y at 4.595%, actively repricing) provides a medium-term NIM tailwind for a bank that is structurally asset-sensitive.
outcome_notes: Catalyst trade (analyst beat + 1.79x volume confirmation) closed flat at schedule with no follow-through. The volume signal did not sustain into a price move, suggesting either the catalyst was already fully priced at entry or liquidity in ABN.AS absorbed the buying without directional resolution.
risks_observed: Volume-confirmed catalyst trades carry the specific risk that the confirming volume *is* the event — once the burst clears, price stalls or fades. Entering after a high-volume beat rather than ahead of it means the informational edge may already be exhausted. A neutral-sector stock-specific trade also lacks a sector tailwind to carry it if the idiosyncratic catalyst loses momentum.

thesis: Technology is Mildly Bullish on the intact AI/industrial capex cycle, and Infineon's European automotive/industrial semiconductor exposure gives it differentiated upside from that thesis versus pure US megacap tech. MACD is strongly positive and expanding (+5.279/+4.669/+0.610); sized smaller to reflect fading 5d sector momentum and the elevated 41.7% 20d run that warrants a wider stop.
outcome_notes: Flat exit at schedule; the AI/industrial capex thesis and strongly positive MACD setup produced no intraday price action. The 41.7% 20-day run context noted at entry appears to have capped short-term momentum — price consolidated rather than extended.
risks_observed: All four basket positions closed at exactly zero P&L on a scheduled exit, which points to a structural issue: the strategy is generating well-reasoned entries but the exit mechanism (time-based schedule) is not aligned with any of the thesis durations — energy macro, defensive rotation, catalyst follow-through, and momentum continuation all operate on different timescales, none of which are a fixed intraday window. The 41.7% prior run in IFX.DE was flagged at entry as a risk and the flat outcome is consistent with a stock that is extended and digesting gains rather than continuing.

thesis: Strongest 5-day return among non-overbought candidates (+14.18%), rising volume (1.14x), and RSI 68.9 leaves room before the danger zone; defensive tobacco is directly aligned with the macro stagflationary rotation favouring consumer staples over rate-sensitives.
outcome_notes: Scheduled close returned exactly zero, meaning price went nowhere from entry to exit — the 5-day momentum in BATS.L had no intraday continuation on the entry session. The defensive-rotation catalyst was either already fully priced at entry or lacked a fresh trigger to push it further on the day.
risks_observed: Entering a name already up 14%+ over five days on a session that delivered flat price action is a warning sign that momentum was exhausted at the point of entry; the scheduled-exit structure means the position couldn't benefit from any multi-day follow-through while still bearing overnight gap risk. All four basket positions closed flat on the same day, suggesting a systemic timing issue rather than a stock-specific one.

thesis: Top 5-day return in the entire candidate set (+14.36%) with price extended well above both MAs and expanding MACD histogram; RSI 74.2 is borderline overbought so stop is set at 1.5× ATR to protect against mean-reversion while the trend runs.
outcome_notes: Zero P&L on a scheduled close; ITRK.L entered with the highest-ranked 5-day return in the set and an RSI already at 74.2, and price action on the entry day produced no extension whatsoever. The momentum signal appears to have been lagging rather than leading — the move had run before entry.
risks_observed: An RSI of 74.2 is the clearest mean-reversion risk in this basket and a scheduled (likely same-day) close window gives insufficient time for trend momentum to overcome the overbought condition; the 1.5× ATR stop was appropriately defensive but the reward setup requires immediate continuation that didn't arrive. This was the highest-risk entry in the basket on the overbought dimension.

thesis: Highest volume ratio in the set (1.79×) confirms institutional accumulation on the back of a Q1 earnings beat and analyst estimate upgrades; RSI 68.0 with a healthy MACD histogram provides clean technical backing for continuation.
outcome_notes: Flat exit at scheduled close; the high volume ratio (1.79×) that justified an institutional-accumulation read did not translate into price continuation on the entry day, suggesting the Q1 earnings catalyst had already been absorbed by the market prior to entry. Post-earnings momentum entries are sensitive to exactly this timing risk.
risks_observed: ABN.AS introduces European financials exposure that behaves differently from the UK consumer and industrials names in the same basket — credit spread widening or ECB commentary can move it independently and in the opposite direction. The earnings-beat thesis has a short shelf life; entering days after the catalyst leaves only technical momentum to carry the trade, which failed here.

thesis: Robust 20-day trend (+16.62%) with above-average volume (1.43×) and RSI 66.3 leaving meaningful headroom before overbought; wider stop accounts for the larger ATR-relative spread typical of lower-priced shares while the 2:1 reward ratio keeps expected value positive.
outcome_notes: Scheduled close at entry price with no movement captured; the strong 20-day trend in CPG.L at a £0.32 handle meant the bid-ask spread likely consumed any marginal intraday drift, and the entry session produced no directional push to overcome that friction. The trend signal was real but the holding window was too short to express it.
risks_observed: At £0.32 per share, the spread is a material drag on the stated 2:1 reward ratio — the effective entry is worse than the mid-price and the effective exit is worse still, meaning the break-even threshold is structurally higher than the model assumes. The wider ATR-adjusted stop needed for low-priced shares also limits position sizing, reducing this name's contribution to basket returns even when the directional call is correct.

thesis: Credit spread dynamics favour HY over IG — HYG 5d -0.85% vs LQD -1.23% (HY-IG diff +0.38pp, risk-on) with no credit-stress signal; IHYG.L holds above both SMA20 and SMA50, RSI 53 non-extended, leaving room to run. Duration exposure avoided entirely: 10Y broke above 4.6% and 30Y at 5.13% makes TLT/IEF toxic in a stagflationary rising-rate regime — credit-only is the only viable bond-cycle expression today.
outcome_notes: The position exited flat at the entry price on a scheduled close — no price movement was captured in either direction. The HY-over-IG relative value thesis never had a chance to express itself intraday; credit spreads either didn't move enough or IHYG.L's illiquidity suppressed any price discovery that occurred in the underlying.
risks_observed: The zero P&L outcome highlights execution risk in a thinly traded LSE-listed ETF: scheduled exits at mid-price in low-volume instruments frequently round-trip to zero net of spread, making the strategy reliant on multi-day holds to realise any edge. The single-name concentration in one HY vehicle with no IG or short-duration hedge means any sudden credit-stress episode (spread blowout, risk-off spike) would hit the full position with no offset.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+12.26% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Price held exactly at the open level through the scheduled exit, generating no intraday signal. After a 12%+ overnight gap — the largest in this basket — the complete absence of movement is itself informative: either liquidity dried up after the open print or the gap was driven by a one-off event that exhausted buying interest immediately.
risks_observed: A double-digit gap on a UK small-cap is a textbook event-driven move with high mean-reversion risk; entering with a fixed scheduled exit and no stop left the position fully exposed to a fade that never materialised here but will in similar setups. Wide bid/ask spreads at this price and volume level can silently erode effective P&L beyond what the nominal 0.00% suggests.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+4.31% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Flat exit at entry price; the moderate gap held but produced no continuation, leaving the momentum thesis neither confirmed nor refuted. Scheduled exit on a day with zero price movement provides no useful signal about whether the prior session's move had any predictive content.
risks_observed: No stop or profit target means the strategy absorbs intraday drawdowns silently — the flat close could mask a significant intraday excursion that recovered by the scheduled exit time. At the basket level, holding three European names simultaneously (TEP.PA, WKL.AS, CAP.PA) creates latent correlation risk if a macro catalyst hits mid-session.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+3.87% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Entry and scheduled exit both at the same price; the gap stalled immediately with no follow-through. Wolters Kluwer's gap was the smallest of the European names in the basket, and the lack of any movement suggests the prior-session move was already fully absorbed at the open.
risks_observed: WKL.AS correlates closely with CAP.PA and other European data/software names also in this basket, meaning the three Paris/Amsterdam positions are not independent bets — a sector-level sell-off would hit all three simultaneously with no diversification benefit.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+3.82% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Price pinned at the round €100.00 level from entry to scheduled exit with no intraday resolution. The round number likely attracted both buyers and sellers in equal measure, suppressing movement and making this day's data point uninformative for evaluating the momentum rule.
risks_observed: Round-number anchoring at €100 can mask intraday volatility that nets to zero by close; the scheduled exit with no intraday monitoring means the strategy would have sat through any dip and recovery without acting. Capgemini's size means it is index-weight sensitive — any passive rebalancing flow on this date could have driven the temporary stability.

thesis: Top previous-day gainer (+3.06% close-to-open). Rule-based control: buying the prior session's strongest movers with no further filter.
outcome_notes: Flat close at the sub-£1 entry price; the gap failed to extend but also held, giving no directional edge. At this price level, a 0.00% nominal outcome may actually represent a small net loss once spread is accounted for.
risks_observed: Sub-penny spread as a fraction of notional is materially higher for JD.L at £0.72 than for the European names in this basket, making transaction costs a disproportionate headwind that the flat P&L figure obscures. The UK-listed name also introduces GBP/EUR cross-rate noise when evaluating basket-level performance against the two Paris and one Amsterdam positions.

thesis: TEP.PA is French gas transmission infrastructure — the most direct UK/EU expression of the oil and gas supply-shock thesis that is the W21 regime's dominant signal. The stock has confirmed the macro call (+31.75% 20d, above both SMAs, expanding MACD), and wider stops are appropriate given the macro play horizon; the 10% TP is conservative relative to the 20d move already seen, targeting the next leg of the energy rotation.

Jupiter Fund Management sits in a macro-neutral sector (Financials) but exhibits the cleanest remaining technical setup — above both SMAs, RSI 61, positive MACD trend — with a 20d uptrend intact; the yield-curve steepening provides a medium-term fundamental tailwind for asset managers with AUM in fixed income, making it a low-conviction but technically sound complement to the TEP.PA energy core.

Oil is the one commodity where the supply shock is the macro story: Iran escalation rhetoric and active Hormuz risk keep the geopolitical premium bid, and the technical trend (above SMA20 $138.57 and SMA50 $128.01, RSI 61.6) confirms momentum rather than just the news spike. Contango drag is irrelevant in a directional move of this magnitude; widening to 8% TP reflects the underlying's elevated volatility (ATR 4.5% of price).
